name: Exclusions from Confidential Information
text: Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available without breach of this Agreement; (b) was known to the Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by the Disclosing Party; or (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to such information.

legal_basis.note: Exclusions make the restriction specific, not absolute — enforceable

invalid_if: The exclusion definition inadvertently covers information intended to be highly confidential or a trade secret.
The exclusions are so broad as to negate the primary purpose of the Non-Disclosure Agreement.
Contradicts specific statutory or regulatory requirements regarding information secrecy.
title: Confidentiality Exclusions
dispute_hotspots: Compelled disclosure — insufficient notice to Disclosing Party
Prior knowledge claim — no contemporaneous record maintained
